$schema_note: Proposed by ExistentialAuthor during the Rev-25 additive-credit rework. PENDING ratification. Reuses the ratified 'transfer' family; one new specific defined here. Thread: inter_chat/Architecture_ExistentialAuthor_cue_level_and_additive_credit.md.

proposed_family_actions.note: English any/some over-translated as an Italian partitive or quantifier in the existential, where the bare noun is required.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Partitive/quantifier for a bare noun (any/some transfer)
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Inserting delle/dei/del/alcune/qualche in an existential where Italian takes the bare noun, because English 'any/some' has no equivalent here. The learner produces ci sono delle domande / non c'è del tempo for the target ci sono domande / non c'è tempo. Distinct from a genuine partitive use elsewhere; here the existential + bare plural (or uncountable) is the required form.
examples: ci sono delle domande (for ci sono domande)
non c'è del tempo (for non c'è tempo)
non ci sono dei problemi (for non ci sono problemi)
ci sono del latte e del caffè (for ci sono latte e caffè)
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: High-value English-transfer pattern that no estate item tested before the Rev-25 rework; Architecture offered to mint on evidence. The five reworked items now carry the guard population. On mint, I tag the over-translation must_not entries (currently noted with the proposed id in their notes) on my next touch.
